text.title: Context Bleed Detection Recipe Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure: probe to measure to score to classify to emit. Each step has typed input/output + a decision gate.

step.name: probe
step.input: session pool to monitor + per-session canary generator + system-prompt snapshot
step.action: Inject a unique canary token into each session's out-of-band instruction slot. Record the per-session canary in a side-channel map.

step.name: measure
step.action: Scan every response for any foreign canary. Independently compute pairwise turn-level embedding distances between sessions active in the same minute.
step.decision-gate: If canary-hit list non-empty - mark candidate cross-tenant. If any pairwise distance < threshold - mark candidate prior-turn.

step.name: score
step.input: candidate list from step 2 + snapshot diff between probe-start and probe-end
step.action: Score each candidate: canary_hit (binary) + distance_value (float) + snapshot_drift (binary). Compose into a single signal record.

step.name: classify
step.action: Apply decision tree (06-decision-tree.xml). Output bleed_type label: cross-tenant / prior-turn / system-drift / clean.
step.decision-gate: If bleed_type=clean and no signal - exit with no-action. Else continue.

step.name: emit
step.input: classified candidates + tenant - session frozen mapping
step.action: Construct incident record per 02-output-contract.xml. Hash all session-ids (SHA-256, 16 chars). Pick scoped kill_switch. Page named on-call owner.
step.output: incident_id, owner, bleed_type, probe_signal, session_hashes, threshold, kill_switch, version=1.0.0, detected_at=now

list.item: Named on-call owner has acknowledged the page
No raw user-ids leaked into the incident
